meta.name: SDQ Strengths and Difficulties Questionnaire
meta.description: The SDQ is a 25-item scale assessing emotional symptoms, conduct problems, hyperactivity/inattention, peer relationship problems, and prosocial behavior in children and adolescents (aged 4-17).
meta.author: Goodman R (1997)
meta.reference: Goodman R. The Strengths and Difficulties Questionnaire: a research note. J Child Psychol Psychiatry. 1997;38(5):581-586.
meta.instruction: For each item, please mark the box that best describes the child's behavior over the last six months.

questions.text: Considerate of other people's feelings.
Restless, overactive, cannot stay still for long.
Often complains of headaches, stomach-aches or sickness.
Shares readily with other children (treats, toys, pencils etc.).
Often has temper tantrums or hot tempers.
Rather solitary, tends to play alone.
Generally obedient, usually does what adults request.
Many worries, often seems worried.
Helpful if someone is hurt, upset or feeling ill.
Constantly fidgeting or squirming.
Has at least one good friend.
Often fights with other children or bullies them.
Often unhappy, down-hearted or tearful.
Generally liked by other children.
Easily distracted, concentration wanders.
Nervous or clingy in new situations, easily loses confidence.
Kind to younger children.
Often lies or cheats.
Picked on or bullied by other children.
Often volunteers to help others (parents, teachers, other children).
Thinks things out before acting.
Steals from home, school or elsewhere.
Gets on better with adults than with other children.
Many fears, easily scared.
Sees tasks through to the end, good attention span.

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Total difficulties score is within normal range (excluding prosocial subscale). This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue monitoring the child's mental health development
Maintain a supportive family communication environment
Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities

ranges.label: Borderline
ranges.description: Total difficulties score is at a borderline level. Monitoring is advised. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Monitor the child's behavioral and emotional changes closely
Maintain communication with school teachers
Consider a more detailed assessment
Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise
Learn simple relaxation and stress-relief techniques

ranges.label: Abnormal
ranges.description: Total difficulties score is elevated. Professional evaluation is recommended. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Consult a child mental health professional
Focus on specific problem dimensions for targeted intervention
Early intervention helps improve outcomes
Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences

emotional.label: Normal
emotional.description: Emotional symptoms within normal range. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties

emotional.label: Borderline
emotional.description: Emotional symptoms at borderline level. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise
Learn simple relaxation and stress-relief techniques
Stay in communication with people you trust
If symptoms persist or worsen, consider consulting a professional
Maintain a positive outlook on life

emotional.label: Abnormal
emotional.description: Elevated emotional symptoms. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties

conduct.label: Normal
conduct.description: Conduct problems within normal range. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties

conduct.label: Borderline
conduct.description: Conduct problems at borderline level. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise
Learn simple relaxation and stress-relief techniques
Stay in communication with people you trust
If symptoms persist or worsen, consider consulting a professional
Maintain a positive outlook on life

conduct.label: Abnormal
conduct.description: Elevated conduct problems. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties

hyperactivity.label: Normal
hyperactivity.description: Hyperactivity/inattention within normal range. This indicates that the relevant behavioral characteristics are within the normal range with no significant functional impact.
suggestions: Understand your behavioral characteristics and leverage your strengths
Maintain your current effective coping strategies
Seek information and support when needed

hyperactivity.label: Borderline
hyperactivity.description: Hyperactivity/inattention at borderline level. Mild behavioral differences are common. Understanding your own patterns can help you adapt more effectively to different environments.
suggestions: Learn about your behavioral patterns and characteristics
Use planners and reminders to help organize daily activities
Communicate your needs to others when necessary
Leverage your unique strengths and creativity

hyperactivity.label: Abnormal
hyperactivity.description: Elevated hyperactivity/inattention. This indicates that the relevant behavioral characteristics are within the normal range with no significant functional impact.
suggestions: Understand your behavioral characteristics and leverage your strengths
Maintain your current effective coping strategies
Seek information and support when needed

peer.label: Normal
peer.description: Peer problems within normal range. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties

peer.label: Borderline
peer.description: Peer problems at borderline level. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise
Learn simple relaxation and stress-relief techniques
Stay in communication with people you trust
If symptoms persist or worsen, consider consulting a professional
Maintain a positive outlook on life

peer.label: Abnormal
peer.description: Elevated peer problems. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties

prosocial.label: Normal
prosocial.description: Prosocial behavior within normal range. This indicates that your social functioning and relationships are in good condition, maintaining satisfactory social connections.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your current social relationships
Keep an open and communicative attitude
Participate in social activities that interest you
Focus on the quality rather than quantity of relationships

prosocial.label: Borderline
prosocial.description: Prosocial behavior at borderline level. Mild social concerns are common in everyday life. Expanding social activities and expressing your feelings may be helpful.
suggestions: Try proactively joining social activities or interest groups
Practice social skills and assertive communication
Use online platforms to expand your social network
Share your feelings with someone you trust
Start with small social interactions and gradually expand

prosocial.label: Abnormal
prosocial.description: Low prosocial behavior. This indicates that your social functioning and relationships are in good condition, maintaining satisfactory social connections.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your current social relationships
Keep an open and communicative attitude
Participate in social activities that interest you
Focus on the quality rather than quantity of relationships
